channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Crowds at Milan Fashion Week Featured Spring Dressing, Italian Style
item.description: At Milan Fashion Week, the streets and runways featured breezy attire and bright pastels that evoked springtime.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha
item.media:description: Square sunglasses complemented the grid print of a jacket seen outside the Boss show.

item.title: The Bizarre Shoe Trend That Failed to Take Off
item.description: “Snoafers,” a hybrid of sneakers and loafers, sounded like a good idea in theory.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: The ‘Staples Baddie’ Makes Office Supplies Seem Whimsical
item.description: Kaeden Rowland’s videos that find whimsy in ballpoint pens and toner are traveling widely.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Epstein, ‘Lolita’ and a Culture of Disembodiment
item.description: Disturbing images released in the Epstein files showing passages from Nabokov’s infamous novel written on bodies exemplify a world where women and girls are treated as objects for consumption.
item.dc:creator: Rhonda Garelick

item.title: At Dior, Clothes That Make Light of Luxury
item.description: It was a tale of two jackets from Jonathan Anderson at Dior and Anthony Vaccarello at Saint Laurent.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Bars on parade: Models stroll down a Dior runway over a pond with faux waterlilies in the Tuileries.

item.title: ‘Love Story’ Fans Can Buy Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s Wardrobe in Online Auction
item.description: Several items worn by Ms. Kennedy, which are now on sale in an online auction, showcase her penchant for a curated, minimalist look.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.category: Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Jeremy Pope Gets His Gold
item.description: For the executive producer and star of “The Beauty” on FX, a golden smile is a homage to his culture, his family and his heritage.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Diamond
item.media:credit: Victor Llorente for The New York Times
item.media:description: “When I wear my grill, it evokes a different type of confidence within me,” Jeremy Pope said.

item.title: Unforgettable SAG Actor Awards 2026 Looks: Teyana Taylor, Emma Stone and More
item.description: Painted torsos, tulip skirts, Jazz Age style and more.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:description: Emma Stone sported a pastel sweater with a spaghetti-strap dress to match.

item.title: Yondr Pouches, a Solution to School Phone Bans, Are No Match for Teens
item.description: In some schools, Yondr, a pouch marketed to keep kids off devices, has proved no match for actual children.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Callie Holtermann
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Tonje Thilesen for The New York Times

item.title: Thaddeus Mosley, Sculptor Who Found Fame in His Last Decade, Dies at 99
item.description: A self-taught artist, he turned reclaimed wood into striking abstract works influenced by Brancusi, Noguchi and African art.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich
item.media:credit: Ross Mantle for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thaddeus Mosley at his studio in Pittsburgh in 2023. Of the racial and economic barriers he faced, he said: “I knew that I wasn’t going to get the same recognition, have the same opportunity. But I tried, because it’s something I wanted to do.”

item.title: What Clothes Feel New Now?
item.description: In Paris, Loewe and Polo bring some much needed color and uplift.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: This Work Zoom Shouldn’t Have Gone X-Rated
item.description: Plus, how to deal when your pal is the toxic one at the office.
item.dc:creator: Max Read

item.title: A Panorama of Reimagined Designs
item.description: A look at design-world events, products and people.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Rustic Farm Weddings, Now Even More Refined
item.description: Say goodbye to Mason jars and hello to Scandinavian-inspired barns. This return to agrarian aesthetics is as refined as it is rustic.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Sullivan

item.title: For These Design Materials, It’s Goodbye and Good Riddance
item.description: Not all acts of extinction are to be regretted.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Treffinger
item.media:description: PFAS, so-called forever chemicals, were developed in the early 20th century, and are the reason Teflon cookware doesn’t stick, why many pizza boxes resist grease and why clothing can be waterproof.

item.title: The New York Building That Couldn’t Be Budged
item.description: An Upper East Side townhouse engulfed by new construction tells a very New York story.
item.dc:creator: Ian Volner
item.media:credit: Katherine Marks for The New York Times
item.media:description: 1301 Third Avenue is sandwiched between an 18-story luxury condominium and a 32-story tower.

item.title: At Givenchy, Sarah Burton Dresses the Complex Woman
item.description: Or Sarah Burton, the brand’s creative director, did, in all her multiple incarnations. Rick Owens took Marlene Dietrich punk.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Stella McCartney Is Awarded the French Medal of Honor
item.description: Stella McCartney commands the axis of cultural power for the French Legion of Honor.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale

item.title: At Some Weddings, Being a Bridesmaid Comes with Strict Rules
item.description: In a TikTok video, an influencer said that she would prefer for bridesmaids not to wear a specific hairstyle to her wedding, igniting a conversation about the strict rules bridesmaids often follow.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: A TikTok debate erupted after a bride said she was banning the slicked-back bun from her bridesmaids’ hairstyles.

item.title: Paul Simon, Whoopi Goldberg and Bill Murray Backstage at the Beacon
item.description: At a gala concert, musicians were supported by star-studded hosts including Whoopi Goldberg and Bill Murray — on cowbell.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul and Nina Westervelt

item.title: From 2004: Estée Lauder, Pursuer of Beauty and Cosmetics Titan, Dies at 97
item.description: Her company, known for its creams, powders, ointments and perfumes, was estimated to be worth about $5 billion when it went public in 1995.
item.dc:creator: Richard Severo
item.media:description: Estée Lauder at her office in 1985. She convinced generations of women determined to maintain a youthful appearance that her beauty creams were “jars of hope.”

item.title: From 2019: Agnès Varda, Influential French New Wave Filmmaker, Dies at 90
item.description: She was closely associated with the film movement known as the New Wave, although her reimagining of cinematic conventions predated it.
item.dc:creator: John Anderson

item.title: Tom Junod Would Like to Tell You About His Father
item.description: A star writer from the heyday of magazines reveals the family secret behind his award-winning stories.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Mark Sommerfeld for The New York Times

item.title: The Allure of Traveling to the French Riviera in the Winter
item.description: A region famous for its sun-drenched climate becomes a refreshing retreat when the summer heat, megayachts and swarms of tourists are gone.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Steinman and Gianni Cipriano

item.title: Reclaimed Trees Get Creative Reuse With New York Heartwoods
item.description: Megan Offner finds creative and comforting ways to salvage doomed trees.
item.dc:creator: Jane Margolies
item.media:description: Megan Offner is the founder of New York Heartwoods. The circular wood products consultancy reclaims trees, providing a more meaningful and sustainable alternative to commercial-grade lumber.

item.title: From Endangered Plants Come Whiffs of Inspiration
item.description: How scientists, conservationists and the fragrance industry are working to revive extinct scents and perpetuate threatened ones.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: At Alaïa, Saying Goodbye With the Perfect Coat
item.description: Assessing Pieter Mulier’s final Alaïa show — and his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Where real luxury is in the fit.

item.title: A Power Outage Made Us Possible
item.description: If he couldn’t see me or my place, maybe he wouldn’t judge me either.
item.dc:creator: William J. O’Brien

item.title: He Grew Up Watching Her on TV. Years Later, She Joined His World.
item.description: Brian Thalman became familiar with Brooke Hyland while watching her on “Dance Moms.” The two met seven years after her final season on the reality TV show.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: They Met in a Chemistry Class, but the Connection Came Later
item.description: Jordan Goldberg and Adam Rossman were friends at the University of Texas at Austin. Years later, after building a life in New York, they married in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: On the Ninth Hole, Her Dad Took a Swing at Playing Cupid
item.description: Taylor Ross’s father asked Noah Koeppel, his new golf buddy’s son, if he wanted to meet his daughter. He was game. She wasn’t.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s Wedding Slip Dress Continues to Shape Modern Bridal Fashion
item.description: Thirty years after Ms. Bessette’s wedding to John F. Kennedy Jr., the FX series “Love Story” is renewing interest, again, in her minimalist style.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

item.title: Their Favorite Projects? Inventing Cocktails and Card Games.
item.description: For Jai Lennard, a photographer, and Alelli Tanghal, an art director, collaboration has long been the foundation of their romance.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: Tom Ford Thrills
item.description: Haider Ackermann serves up what is likely the show of the season.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: At Tom Ford, Haider Ackermann Dresses Adult Desire
item.description: At Tom Ford, Haider Ackermann explores what might happen just before suits come undone.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: The Boys in Trueblood Came to Rock
item.description: The four teenage brothers mostly play the oldies. (To them, the oldies are Coldplay and Maroon 5.)
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Elinor Kry

item.title: The ‘Mona Lisa’ as You’ve Never Seen Her
item.description: With its ambitious program of designer collaborations, the Louvre is making a new fashion statement.
item.dc:creator: Tina Isaac-Goizé

item.title: The Villa Beer in Vienna Opens as a Museum and Cultural center
item.description: The newly renovated Villa Beer in Vienna is a modernist masterpiece with a dark past.
item.dc:creator: Eve M. Kahn
item.media:credit: Andreas Meichsner for The New York Times
item.media:description: After decades of moldering, the Villa Beer property opens as a museum and cultural center, with some of time’s ravages on view.

item.title: Christian Astuguevieille, 79, Dies; Created Strange Scents and Enigmatic Objects
item.description: For Commes des Garçons, he designed improbable perfumes that conjured burning rubber and cars leaking oil. His uncanny art pieces were equally contrarian.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green
item.media:credit: Marie Pierre Morel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Christian Astuguevieille at home in Paris in 1995, with his chests of knotted and tufted cord. “Altering and deforming the original use of things is important to me,” he told The New York Times in 2011. “I love to mystify.”

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘I Brought Only Sex and Chaos’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: Why Do All Fashion Designers Dress Alike?
item.description: Navy sweaters. Dark pants. Jeans. What the consistency of the industry’s top designers says about their collections (and how we should receive them).
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Clockwise from top left, Andreas Solaro/AFP — Getty Images; Luca Bruno/Associated Press; Miguel Medina/AFP — Getty Images; Hoda Davaine/Getty Images
item.media:description: Clockwise from top left: The designer bow uniform, as modeled by Maximilian Davis at Ferragamo; Raf Simons at Prada; Louise Trotter at Bottega Veneta; and Erdem Moralioglu.

item.title: To Many, the Buildings Are Incredible Hulks. They’re Doomed Anyway.
item.description: Why the country is quick to tear down its modern architectural masterpieces.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Pollock

item.title: My Boss Didn’t Care That My Mother Was Dying. Help!
item.description: A reader can hardly stand to look at a superior after finding her recent anguish met with talk of “contractual obligations.”
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Parisians Have Given In to the Lunch Salad
item.description: Office workers in the French capital, renowned for taking luxuriously long midday meals, have developed an appetite for the on-the-go slop bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy

item.title: 6 Design Products That May Be the Last of Their Kind
item.description: Many artisanal practices are hanging by a thread, but there is still a chance to enjoy them.
item.dc:creator: Diana Budds
item.media:description: Pei-Ching Hsiao started Taiwan-Lantern after learning about what is reputedly the last traditional lantern maker in her home country: a workshop in the forests of Nantou, Taiwan.

item.title: Ceramics, Once the Lifeblood of Mexican Artisans, Are Under Pressure
item.description: In the state of Michoacán, industrial agriculture and organized crime threaten a cottage industry.
item.dc:creator: Michael Snyder and César Rodríguez

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Issey Miyake and Gucci
item.description: A writer’s bold attire reflected a taste for wearing color.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: These Men Want to Know Their ‘Sexual Market Value’
item.description: The idea of putting a dollar figure on attractiveness has bubbled up from obscure corners of the internet.
item.dc:creator: T.M. Brown

item.title: When Life Gave Her Ageism, Sari Botton Created ‘Oldster’
item.description: Sari Botton started a Substack about getting older after finding employers were reluctant to hire her, a middle-aged woman. With more than 70,000 subscribers, she has clearly struck a nerve.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat
item.media:credit: Kate Warren for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sari Botton, who edits two popular newsletters on Substack, Oldster and Memoir Land, at her home in Kingston, N.Y.

item.title: On ‘Industry,’ Who Decides What Exploitation Looks Like?
item.description: “Industry” finished up its fourth season on Sunday night, and there is much to discuss.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

item.title: Zendaya and Tom Holland Are Married, Stylist Law Roach Says
item.description: The subject was brought up during the Actor Awards, to which Zendaya’s longtime stylist replied, “It’s very true.”
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan
item.media:description: Husband and wife?

item.title: An ‘American Gigolo’ in Milan
item.description: At a Giorgio Armani exhibition, an ensemble worn by Richard Gere taught a lesson about what makes a great designer. Also, cutout beanies, more men’s Chanel and a very good pair of jeans.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: The Giorgio Arman exhibition at the Pinacoteca di Brera art museum in Milan.

item.title: SAG Actor Awards Red Carpet 2026: See Photos of the Stars’ Looks
item.description: Timothée Chalamet, Teyana Taylor and Chase Infiniti walked the red carpet for the Screen Actor Guild’s Actor Awards on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:description: Timothée Chalamet, nominated for best actor in a lead role for “Marty Supreme.”

item.title: Why Couples Are Choosing Domestic Partnership Over Marriage
item.description: While numbers remain relatively small, more couples are choosing legal partnerships over marriage. Some are marking the moment with celebrations that look and feel like weddings.
item.dc:creator: Alix Strauss

item.title: In a New ‘Narrative Cookbook,’ Tanya Bush Makes a Case for the Pre-Recipe Preamble
item.description: Tanya Bush, a writer and pastry chef in Brooklyn, makes a case for taking the scenic route to the recipe in a new “narrative cookbook.”
item.dc:creator: Maggie Lange and Evelyn Freja
item.media:description: In her new book, “Will This Make You Happy,” Ms. Bush doesn’t shy away from recounting the failures in her baking life (or, for that matter, her real life).

item.title: How a ‘Smut Renaissance’ Is Reshaping Our Digital Consumption and Desires
item.description: On TV, in film and in the pages of popular novels, smut is more plentiful and personal than ever. What happens when our screens are steamier than our bedrooms?
item.dc:creator: Amanda Hess

item.title: At Milan Fashion Week, Blanket Statements From Jil Sander and Bottega Veneta
item.description: Blanket statements were the biggest trend in Milan. Jil Sander led the way; Bottega Veneta got hairy.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
